title: jasminum nudiflorum var. aureum
intro: Jasminum nudiflorum var. aureum, also known as the golden winter jasmine, is a flowering evergreen vine native to Asia, characterized by its golden-yellow flowers that bloom from December to April. The golden winter jasmine has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving stress, anxiety, and insomnia, as well as acting as a natural anti-depressant. In herbal preparations, the golden winter jasmine is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
